extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu bridged a 30km solo by FABBRO Matteo in the final 500m of the Loreto wall, beating GUERREIRO Ruben by 4 seconds.
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL Mathieu held back while GUERREIRO Ruben chased, then launched final 500m when gradient steepest—conservation trumped early pursuit
FABBRO Matteo's 30km solo nearly succeeded because GC teams deferred to VAN DER POEL rather than organizing chase themselves
KELDERMAN Wilco finished 4th despite no lead-out, suggesting Sunweb prioritized GC positioning over sprint collaboration on this finish
YATES Simon's 16-second margin over MAJKA Rafał entering TT reveals defensive riding—he admitted wanting more buffer, signaling vulnerability

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: Won two consecutive stages with different tactical approaches, showing versatility and repeatedly timed final accelerations perfectly on steep gradients
rider_signals.FABBRO Matteo: positive: Nearly held 30km solo on hot day after 'one and a half hour' of attacks, indicating exceptional endurance reserves
rider_signals.GUERREIRO Ruben: positive: Distanced the chasers before VAN DER POEL's final surge, confirming climbing superiority over most classics riders
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Admitted 16-second GC lead feels insufficient before TT, suggesting mental weakness or awareness of poor time trialing form
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Fourth place without team support on punchy finish indicates improving condition late in stage race
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Finished in main GC group 39 seconds down, relies entirely on TT to salvage podium
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: neutral: Only 16 seconds behind YATES entering TT, but commentary implies time trial disadvantage remains
